But then we want to be able to capture this entire interaction, like, which was, like, my input as an editor, what I did, what context to use.
And, you know, and over time, that's, like, the most, we call it the messy middle.
This messy middle is the most important, but the messy middle is not just, like, a data labeling project off in a vacuum.
It's, like, the way people work today is with AI.
So, you want to capture those interactions with AI, but you don't want

What we found is like it doesn't scale.

So we're able to build these really complex, really powerful workflows in minutes now and learn from them and iterate with them, you know, and it's getting into a place where like what we're building towards is kind of this almost like, I don't want say autonomous, but like self-healing or self-improving loop where you have the human feedback and based on the outputs, the feedback from the outputs, you can go back to these components of these pieces and the coding agent can learn what it needs to do differently.
